title: transport: reconnect drops in-flight requests silently on channel overflow
description: RelayConnRetry.tx holds 64 requests. During a disconnect, requests sent to tx accumulate. If the buffer fills (> 64 outstanding), excess requests are silently dropped. The struct-level comment mentions this but the drop is invisible to callers — send() on an mpsc::Sender with capacity succeeds (returns Ok) even though the request will be dropped when the channel is drained. Actually: send() blocks/errors when the channel is full. But the connection_manager drains out_rx only while connected; during reconnect the channel fills and callers' send() will return Err (channel full), which callers are not required to check. A caller that ignores the Err loses the request silently. The issue is that the API contract ('requests sent while disconnected accumulate') is only true up to 64 requests, and overflow is not signalled as a distinct error type.
